---
title: "Welcome to The Consortium Blog"
author: "The Consortium"
date: "2026-05-08"
categories: [meta, announcement]
description: "Introducing our shared space for open, reproducible scholarly communication."
---

## Hello from The Consortium

We are excited to launch The Consortium Blog — a shared, open-access space
where members of our research group publish methods notes, data explorations,
replications, and tutorials.

## What to expect

Posts on this blog fall into a few recurring categories:

- **Methods notes** — focused walkthroughs of statistical or computational
  approaches, including worked examples
- **Data explorations** — descriptive and exploratory analyses using public
  or shared datasets, with fully reproducible code
- **Replications** — transparent attempts to reproduce findings from
  published work, noting where results align and where they diverge
- **Tutorials** — practical, step-by-step guides for tools, packages, and
  research workflows

All posts are written in [Quarto](https://quarto.org), rendered to HTML,
and published via Netlify. Code is folded by default — click **Code** on
any chunk to expand it.

## How posts are added

Every post lives in a branch named `post/short-description`. When a draft
is ready for review, the author opens a pull request on GitHub. Netlify
automatically generates a deploy-preview link in the PR so collaborators
can read the rendered post before it merges.

See [CONTRIBUTING.md](../../CONTRIBUTING.md) for the full step-by-step
workflow, including folder naming conventions and required front matter.

We look forward to sharing work in progress with you.
